expectations.text: rewrite.md contains AT LEAST THREE distinct specific numeric measurements (e.g. '120ms', '3x', '40%', '500 QPS', a specific year like '2024-Q3', a count like '47 endpoints'). Synonyms like 'considerably faster' or 'dramatically improved' do NOT count — the assertion requires actual digits in three different places. Baseline rewrites typically include 1-2 numbers; this assertion fails when the rewrite is under-specified.
expectations.evidence: No rewrite.md was produced. The executor aborted with 'Prompt is too long' (output.txt contains only that string). Timing shows 0 tokens used, confirming the run never executed.

expectations.text: rewrite.md names AT LEAST TWO specific named entities — proper-noun examples: a database product (Postgres / MySQL / Redis / DynamoDB / Aurora), a specific tool/version (pgBouncer 1.21, Datadog, New Relic), an explicit endpoint path (/orders, /api/v2/checkout, GraphQL field name), or a specific framework/library by name. Generic terms ('the database', 'the cache', 'queries', 'the system', 'most-trafficked endpoints', 'the reporting layer', 'connection pooling') do NOT count. Baseline rewrites rarely invent multiple proper nouns when none were in the source.
expectations.evidence: No rewrite.md was produced. Run failed before any output was generated.

expectations.text: rewrite.md demonstrates the user-side impact rather than asserting it: contains AT LEAST ONE specific user-side metric, named cohort, or named funnel step — examples that count: a measured outcome ('checkout completion rose from 71% to 79%', 'support tickets fell 40%', 'bounce rate dropped from 12% to 4%'), OR a specific named cohort/funnel ('paying customers on the Pro plan', 'mobile checkout funnel step 3', 'enterprise SSO sign-in'). The literal phrase 'positive impact' / 'user experience' / 'improved UX' / 'better experience' does NOT count — those are abstract assertions. Baseline rewrites generally describe impact in abstract terms; the skill explicitly teaches naming the specific user behavior change.
expectations.evidence: No rewrite.md was produced. Run failed before any output was generated.

notes: Eval prompt exceeds model context limit — the with_skill run includes the full skill text which may push the prompt over the token limit. Consider trimming the eval prompt or the skill body.

expectations.text: hook.md commits to AT LEAST ONE of these named hook types in its opening 80 words, identifiable by structural pattern: (a) MISCONCEPTION — first 2 sentences contain a stated conventional view followed by a literal reversal word ('but', 'however', 'actually', 'except', 'the truth is', 'turns out'); (b) IN-MEDIAS-RES — opens with a specific time marker (e.g. '02:47 AM', 'last Tuesday', 'in Q3 2024') AND a specific actor or place in the same or adjacent sentence; (c) FAILED-EXPECTATION — opens with at least 3 sentences forming a 'we did X / we expected Y / we got Z' pattern (3 short clauses, comma- or period-separated, containing 'expected'/'thought'/'assumed' contrasted with 'instead'/'actually'/'turned out'); (d) SURPRISING-FACT — opens with a number paired with a 'not X, not Y' explicit comparison pattern; (e) ARTIFACT-LED — first 50 words contain a literal code block, terminal-output snippet, or specific quoted error message. A generic essay opener about CI in general does NOT satisfy any of these.
expectations.evidence: Executor failed with API error 400 'Prompt is too long' before producing any output. No hook.md was created. Output file contains only 'Prompt is too long'.

expectations.text: hook.md contains AT LEAST TWO distinct specific numbers paired in the same paragraph — must be two genuinely different quantitative measures (e.g. duration + frequency: '12 min × 80 PRs/week'; cost + timeframe: '$200K/quarter for 18 months'; count + percentage: '47 services with 80% test coverage'). Two instances of the same measure (e.g. two durations '12 minutes' and '15 minutes') count as ONE distinct measure. Baseline hooks typically include one number; the skill explicitly teaches stacking compound concreteness.
expectations.evidence: Executor failed with API error 400 'Prompt is too long' before producing any output. No hook.md was created.

expectations.text: hook.md respects Sugarman slippery-slide sentence discipline — must satisfy ALL THREE: (a) the FIRST sentence is ≤25 words, (b) NO single sentence in the hook exceeds 35 words, (c) at least ONE sentence in the hook is ≤8 words (a punch sentence used as a pivot). Baseline hooks tend to open with a 25-40 word balanced sentence and avoid short-punch sentences.
expectations.evidence: Executor failed with API error 400 'Prompt is too long' before producing any output. No hook.md was created.

notes: Run failed due to prompt being too long — the eval prompt (with skill context injected) exceeded the model's context limit. Consider reducing skill content or splitting the eval.
